title: pilosocereus polygonus subsp. curtisii
intro: Pilosocereus polygonus subsp. curtisii, also known as Curtis' Pincushion Cactus, is a rare and endemic species of cactus native to the island of Réunion in the Indian Ocean. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat a variety of health conditions. The health benefits of Pilosocereus polygonus subsp. curtisii include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and combating infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's stems can be dried and ground into a powder, which can be used to make teas, ointments, and salves.
